manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spine, missile, luck, ground, moon, train, string, line, post.
Your opponent's team words are: stadium, sub, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon.
Distractor words are: greece, strike, satellite.
The kill words are: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue related to some of our team's words. Must avoid being one of the words in lists or contain parts. Provide clue and targets list. Should be safe (no opponent/distractor/kill words). Provide maybe clue "rail" relates to train, line, maybe ground? But must not be same as any list words. "rail" is not in lists. Could cover train, line, maybe spine (rail spine?), missile? Maybe "track" relates to train, line, ground? Track not in list. Could also include "orbit" for moon, missile, line? Not. Let's think of a clue that covers multiple: "metal" maybe missile, string (metal string?) not good.

Goal: single-word clue, list of target words separated by commas. Provide maybe "transport" relates to train, line? But also ground? Hmm.

We can choose any number of target words. Safer to give clue for 2-3 words. Provide clue "railway" but contains "rail"? It's fine as not in list. But "railway" has "rail" which is not a word in list. It relates to train, line, ground? Could be train, line. Also "post" maybe? Not.

Alternatively clue "gravity" relates to moon, ground, spine? Might be ambiguous.

We must avoid opponent words: stadium, sub, dinosaur, nut, olive, witch, chair, lemon. Also distractor: greece, strike, satellite. Kill: shark, shot, tag, cloak, tablet.

Clue cannot contain parts of them? "no parts of them" means cannot contain substring of any word? It says cannot contain parts of them. Safer to avoid any substring of any word lists. So "railway" doesn't contain stadium, sub, etc. Fine.

Clue must not be one of the words in the lists. "railway" not in any.

We need list of target words that clue clearly relates to. Let's choose "train" and "line" maybe also "ground"? Not clearly.

Better clue "track" relates to train and line. Also maybe ground (track on ground). It's okay.

Check substrings: "track" contains "rack" not in list. Fine.

Provide:

CLUE: track
TARGETS: train,line

Make sure not referencing any other. That seems good.

We could add "ground" but may be ambiguous with other words.

I'll output
